passage: “Lines Written in Early Spring” is a 1798 poem by William Wordsworth. In the poem, the speaker describes having contradictory feelings while experiencing the sights and sounds of a spring day: _______
stem: Which quotation from “Lines Written in Early Spring” most effectively illustrates the claim?
choices: “Through primrose-tufts, in that sweet bower, / The periwinkle trail’d its wreathes; / And ’tis my faith that every flower / Enjoys the air it breathes.”
“The budding twigs spread out their fan, / To catch the breezy air; / And I must think, do all I can, / That there was pleasure there.”
“The birds around me hopp’d and play’d: / Their thoughts I cannot measure, / But the least motion which they made, / It seem’d a thrill of pleasure.”
“I heard a thousand blended notes, / While in a grove I [sat] reclined, / In that sweet mood when pleasant thoughts / Bring sad thoughts to the mind.”
answer: “I heard a thousand blended notes, / While in a grove I [sat] reclined, / In that sweet mood when pleasant thoughts / Bring sad thoughts to the mind.”
explanation: This quotation directly pairs opposing emotions—the speaker's “pleasant thoughts” also “bring sad thoughts to the mind”—illustrating the contradictory feelings described in the claim. The other options depict flowers, twigs, or birds seeming to feel pleasure but show no conflicting emotions in the speaker.
